explanation: This command disables all Windows Firewall profiles using PowerShell. It matches the Sigma rule by including the required command-line arguments in a single line.
This command uses quotes to encapsulate the command line, which is a safe mutation technique (insertion). It still matches the Sigma rule because the content inside the quotes is unchanged.
This is the same as the first command but without any mutation. It is included to show the baseline command that matches the Sigma rule.
This command specifies individual profiles instead of using the -All flag. It matches the Sigma rule by including the required command-line arguments.
This command uses the -Command flag to execute the PowerShell command. It matches the Sigma rule by including the required command-line arguments.
